info.title: Firecrawl API
info.description: API for interacting with Firecrawl services to perform web scraping and crawling tasks.

value.name: Blog monitor
value.goal: Notify me when a new blog post is published or the headline changes

crawlMonitor.summary: Crawl a site on a cron schedule

value.name: Docs monitor
value.goal: Notify me when docs pages add, remove, or materially change API behavior

url.description: The URL to send the webhook to. This will trigger for batch scrape started (batch_scrape.started), every page scraped (batch_scrape.page) and when the batch scrape is completed (batch_scrape.completed or batch_scrape.failed). The response will be the same as the `/scrape` endpoint.

headers.description: Headers to send to the webhook URL.

metadata.description: Custom metadata that will be included in all webhook payloads for this crawl

events.description: Type of events that should be sent to the webhook URL. (default: all)

maxConcurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrent scrapes. This parameter allows you to set a concurrency limit for this batch scrape. If not specified, the batch scrape adheres to your team's concurrency limit.

ignoreInvalidURLs.description: If invalid URLs are specified in the urls array, they will be ignored. Instead of them failing the entire request, a batch scrape using the remaining valid URLs will be created, and the invalid URLs will be returned in the invalidURLs field of the response.

zeroDataRetention.description: If true, this will enable zero data retention for this batch scrape. To enable this feature, please contact help@firecrawl.dev

post.summary: Crawl multiple URLs based on options

url.description: The base URL to start crawling from

prompt.description: A prompt to use to generate the crawler options (all the parameters below) from natural language. Explicitly set parameters will override the generated equivalents.

excludePaths.description: URL pathname regex patterns that exclude matching URLs from the crawl. For example, if you set "excludePaths": ["blog/.*"] for the base URL firecrawl.dev, any results matching that pattern will be excluded, such as https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/firecrawl-launch-week-1-recap.

includePaths.description: URL pathname regex patterns that include matching URLs in the crawl. Only the paths that match the specified patterns will be included in the response. Note: the starting URL is also checked against these patterns — if it does not match, the crawl may return 0 pages. For example, if you set "includePaths": ["blog/.*"] for the base URL firecrawl.dev/blog, only pages under /blog/ will be included in the results, such as https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/firecrawl-launch-week-1-recap.

maxDiscoveryDepth.description: Maximum depth to crawl based on discovery order. The root site and sitemapped pages has a discovery depth of 0. For example, if you set it to 1, and you set `sitemap: 'skip'`, you will only crawl the entered URL and all URLs that are linked on that page.

sitemap.description: Sitemap mode when crawling. If you set it to 'skip', the crawler will ignore the website sitemap and only crawl the entered URL and discover pages from there onwards. If you set it to 'only', the crawler will only crawl URLs from the sitemap (plus the start URL) and will not discover links from HTML.

ignoreQueryParameters.description: Do not re-scrape the same path with different (or none) query parameters

regexOnFullURL.description: When true, includePaths and excludePaths regex patterns are matched against the full URL (including query parameters) instead of just the URL pathname. Useful when you need to filter URLs based on query strings.

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to crawl. Default limit is 10000.

crawlEntireDomain.description:
Allows the crawler to follow internal links to sibling or parent URLs, not just child paths.

false: Only crawls deeper (child) URLs.
→ e.g. /features/feature-1 → /features/feature-1/tips ✅
→ Won't follow /pricing or / ❌

true: Crawls any internal links, including siblings and parents.
→ e.g. /features/feature-1 → /pricing, /, etc. ✅

Use true for broader internal coverage beyond nested paths.

allowExternalLinks.description: Allows the crawler to follow links to external websites.

allowSubdomains.description: Allows the crawler to follow links to subdomains of the main domain.

ignoreRobotsTxt.description: Ignore the website's robots.txt rules. Enterprise only — contact support@firecrawl.com to enable.

robotsUserAgent.description: Custom User-Agent string for robots.txt evaluation. When set, robots.txt is fetched with this User-Agent and allow/disallow rules are matched against it instead of the default. Enterprise only — contact support@firecrawl.com to enable.

delay.description: Delay in seconds between scrapes. This helps respect website rate limits. Setting this forces concurrency to 1.

maxConcurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrent scrapes. This parameter allows you to set a concurrency limit for this crawl. If not specified, the crawl adheres to your team's concurrency limit.

url.description: The URL to send the webhook to. This will trigger for crawl started (crawl.started), every page crawled (crawl.page) and when the crawl is completed (crawl.completed or crawl.failed). The response will be the same as the `/scrape` endpoint.

headers.description: Headers to send to the webhook URL.

metadata.description: Custom metadata that will be included in all webhook payloads for this crawl

events.description: Type of events that should be sent to the webhook URL. (default: all)

zeroDataRetention.description: If true, this will enable zero data retention for this crawl. To enable this feature, please contact help@firecrawl.dev

error.example: Payment required to access this resource.
Request rate limit exceeded. Please wait and try again later.
An unexpected error occurred on the server.

prompt.description: Natural language prompt describing what you want to crawl

200.description: Successful response with generated crawl parameters

crawlEntireDomain.description: Whether to crawl the entire domain

robotsUserAgent.description: Custom User-Agent string used for robots.txt evaluation

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to crawl

post.summary: Map multiple URLs based on options

url.description: The base URL to start crawling from

search.description: Specify a search query to order the results by relevance. Example: 'blog' will return URLs that contain the word 'blog' in the URL ordered by relevance.

sitemap.description: Sitemap mode when mapping. If you set it to `skip`, the sitemap won't be used to find URLs. If you set it to `only`, only URLs that are in the sitemap will be returned. By default (`include`), the sitemap and other methods will be used together to find URLs.

ignoreQueryParameters.description: Do not return URLs with query parameters

ignoreCache.description: Bypass the sitemap cache to retrieve fresh URLs. Sitemap data is cached for up to 7 days; use this parameter when your sitemap has been recently updated.

limit.description: Maximum number of links to return

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds. There is no timeout by default.

location.description: Location settings for the request. When specified, this will use an appropriate proxy if available and emulate the corresponding language and timezone settings. Defaults to 'US' if not specified.

languages.description: Preferred languages and locales for the request in order of priority. Defaults to the language of the specified location. See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Accept-Language

error.example: Payment required to access this resource.

post.summary: Extract structured data from pages using LLMs

items.description: The URLs to extract data from. URLs should be in glob format.

prompt.description: Prompt to guide the extraction process

schema.description: Schema to define the structure of the extracted data. Must conform to [JSON Schema](https://json-schema.org/).

enableWebSearch.description: When true, the extraction will use web search to find additional data

ignoreSitemap.description: When true, sitemap.xml files will be ignored during website scanning

includeSubdomains.description: When true, subdomains of the provided URLs will also be scanned

showSources.description: When true, the sources used to extract the data will be included in the response as `sources` key

ignoreInvalidURLs.description: If invalid URLs are specified in the urls array, they will be ignored. Instead of them failing the entire request, an extract using the remaining valid URLs will be performed, and the invalid URLs will be returned in the invalidURLs field of the response.

error.example: Invalid input data.
An unexpected error occurred on the server.

post.summary: Start an agent task for agentic data extraction

urls.description: Optional list of URLs to constrain the agent to

schema.description: Optional JSON schema to structure the extracted data

maxCredits.description: Maximum credits to spend on this agent task. Defaults to 2500 if not set. Values above 2,500 are always billed as paid requests.

strictConstrainToURLs.description: If true, agent will only visit URLs provided in the urls array

model.description: The model to use for the agent task. spark-1-mini (default) is 60% cheaper, spark-1-pro offers higher accuracy for complex tasks

get.summary: Metrics about your team's scrape queue

jobsInQueue.description: Number of jobs currently in your queue

maxConcurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrent active jobs based on your plan

mostRecentSuccess.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful job

get.description: Lists your team's recent API activity from the last 24 hours. Returns metadata about each job including the job ID, which can be used with the corresponding GET endpoint (e.g. GET /crawl/{id}) to retrieve full results. Supports cursor-based pagination and filtering by endpoint.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: Filter by endpoint

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results per page

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the cursor value from the previous response.

id.description: The job ID. Use this with the corresponding GET endpoint to retrieve results.

endpoint.description: The endpoint used for this job

api_version.description: The API version used for this request

target.description: The URL or query that was submitted

cursor.description: Cursor to use for the next page. Null if there are no more results.

has_more.description: Whether there are more results available

post.summary: Search and optionally scrape search results

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (per source type when using multiple sources)

tbs.description: Time-based search parameter. Supports predefined time ranges (`qdr:h`, `qdr:d`, `qdr:w`, `qdr:m`, `qdr:y`), custom date ranges (`cdr:1,cd_min:MM/DD/YYYY,cd_max:MM/DD/YYYY`), and sort by date (`sbd:1`). Values can be combined, e.g. `sbd:1,qdr:w`.

sources.description: Sources to search. Will determine the arrays available in the response. Defaults to ['web'].

categories.description: Categories to filter results by. Defaults to [], which means results will not be filtered by any categories.

includeDomains.description: Restricts search results to the specified domains. Domains should be hostnames only, without protocol or path. Cannot be used with excludeDomains.

excludeDomains.description: Excludes search results from the specified domains. Domains should be hostnames only, without protocol or path. Cannot be used with includeDomains.

tbs.description: Time-based search parameter. Supports predefined time ranges (`qdr:h`, `qdr:d`, `qdr:w`, `qdr:m`, `qdr:y`), custom date ranges (`cdr:1,cd_min:MM/DD/YYYY,cd_max:MM/DD/YYYY`), and sort by date (`sbd:1`). Values can be combined, e.g. `sbd:1,qdr:w`.

location.description: Location parameter for search results (e.g. `San Francisco,California,United States`). For best results, set both this and the `country` parameter.

country.description: ISO country code for geo-targeting search results (e.g. `US`). For best results, set both this and the `location` parameter.

ignoreInvalidURLs.description: Excludes URLs from the search results that are invalid for other Firecrawl endpoints. This helps reduce errors if you are piping data from search into other Firecrawl API endpoints.

enterprise.description: Enterprise search options for Zero Data Retention (ZDR). Use `["zdr"]` for end-to-end ZDR (10 credits / 10 results) or `["anon"]` for anonymized ZDR (2 credits / 10 results). Must be enabled for your team.

markdown.description: Markdown content if scraping was requested

html.description: HTML content if requested in formats

rawHtml.description: Raw HTML content if requested in formats

links.description: Links found if requested in formats

screenshot.description: Screenshot URL if requested in formats. Screenshots expire after 24 hours and can no longer be downloaded.

audio.description: Signed URL to the extracted MP3 audio file if `audio` is in `formats`. The signed URL expires after 1 hour.

video.description: Signed URL to the extracted video file if `video` is in `formats`. The signed URL expires after 1 hour.

sourceURL.description: The original URL that was requested. May differ from the page's final URL if redirects occurred.

url.description: The final URL of the page after all redirects have been followed.

markdown.description: Markdown content if scraping was requested

html.description: HTML content if requested in formats

rawHtml.description: Raw HTML content if requested in formats

links.description: Links found if requested in formats

screenshot.description: Screenshot URL if requested in formats. Screenshots expire after 24 hours and can no longer be downloaded.

audio.description: Signed URL to the extracted MP3 audio file if `audio` is in `formats`. The signed URL expires after 1 hour.

video.description: Signed URL to the extracted video file if `video` is in `formats`. The signed URL expires after 1 hour.

sourceURL.description: The original URL that was requested. May differ from the page's final URL if redirects occurred.

url.description: The final URL of the page after all redirects have been followed.

data.description: The search results. The arrays available will depend on the sources you specified in the request. By default, the `web` array will be returned.

warning.description: Warning message if any issues occurred

id.description: The ID of the search job

creditsUsed.description: The number of credits used for the search

An unexpected error occurred on the server.

ttl.description: Total time-to-live in seconds for the browser session

activityTtl.description: Time in seconds before the session is destroyed due to inactivity

streamWebView.description: Whether to stream a live view of the browser

profile.description: Enable persistent storage across browser sessions. Data saved in one session can be loaded in a later session using the same name.

name.description: A name for the profile. Sessions with the same name share storage.

saveChanges.description: When true, browser state is saved back to the profile on close. Set to false to load existing data without writing. Multiple non-saving sessions are allowed but only one saving session at a time.

cdpUrl.description: WebSocket URL for Chrome DevTools Protocol access

liveViewUrl.description: URL to view the browser session in real time

interactiveLiveViewUrl.description: URL to interact with the browser session in real time (click, type, scroll)

expiresAt.description: When the session will expire based on TTL

error.example: Payment required to access this resource.

post.summary: Submit feedback for a search job

post.description: Diagnose Firecrawl job, account, and API usage issues with an AI support agent.
Answer Firecrawl documentation questions using the public docs corpus.

MonitorSchedule.description: Schedule for monitor checks. Provide either `cron` or `text`.

cron.description: Five-field cron expression. Minimum interval is 15 minutes.

text.description: Natural language schedule. Supported examples include `every 30 minutes`, `every 15 minutes starting at :07`, `hourly`, `every 2 hours`, `daily`, `daily at 9:00`, `daily at 9am`, `daily at 5:30 PM`, and `weekly`.

MonitorWebhook.description: Webhook destination for monitor page and check completion events.

url.description: The URL to send monitor webhooks to.

headers.description: Headers to send to the webhook URL.

metadata.description: Custom metadata included in webhook payloads.

events.description: Monitor webhook events to receive. Defaults to all monitor events.

includeDiffs.description: Include changed page details in email summaries.

id.description: Optional stable ID for this target. Generated if omitted.
Optional stable ID for this target. Generated if omitted.

goal.description: Plain-language goal used to judge whether changed pages are meaningful. If provided and `judgeEnabled` is omitted, judging is enabled automatically.

judgeEnabled.description: Whether to judge changed pages against `goal`. Requires a non-empty `goal` to run.

MonitorUpdateRequest.description: Partial monitor update payload. Include at least one field.

goal.description: Plain-language goal used to judge whether changed pages are meaningful. If provided and `judgeEnabled` is omitted, judging is enabled automatically.

judgeEnabled.description: Whether to judge changed pages against `goal`. Requires a non-empty `goal` to run.

estimatedCreditsPerMonth.description: Upper-bound monthly credit estimate. When judging is enabled, actual usage may be lower because judge credits are only charged for changed pages that are judged.

estimatedCredits.description: Upper-bound credits reserved for this check before Firecrawl knows how many pages changed and require judging.

actualCredits.description: Final credits charged for this check after scrapes, crawls, and any changed-page judge calls complete.

diff.description: Inline diff artifact when the page changed. The shape depends on what the monitor's scrapeOptions.formats asked for. Markdown-only monitors populate both `text` (unified diff) and `json` (parseDiff AST). JSON-extraction monitors populate `json` as a per-field `{previous, current}` map keyed by JSON path. Mixed-mode monitors (`changeTracking` with both `json` and `git-diff` modes) populate both `text` (markdown sidecar) and `json` (per-field diff).

text.description: Unified markdown diff. Present on markdown-only and mixed-mode monitors.

json.description: For markdown-only monitors, a parseDiff AST `{ files: [...] }`. For JSON-extraction (and mixed-mode) monitors, a per-field `{ previous, current }` map keyed by the JSON path into the extraction (e.g. `plans[0].price`).

snapshot.description: Snapshot of the current JSON extraction at this run. Present on JSON-extraction and mixed-mode monitors; absent for markdown-only monitors.

json.description: The full structured JSON extracted on this run, matching the schema/prompt declared on the target's `changeTracking` format.

meaningful.description: Whether the changed page is meaningful for the monitor goal.

meaningfulChanges.description: Goal-relevant changes selected by the judge from the page diff.

next.description: URL to fetch the next page of monitor check page results, if any.
URL to fetch the next page of monitor check page results, if any.

fullPage.description: Whether to capture a full-page screenshot (ignores viewport.height) or limit to the current viewport.

quality.description: The quality of the screenshot, from 1 to 100. 100 is the highest quality.

width.description: The width of the viewport in pixels

height.description: The height of the viewport in pixels

schema.description: The schema to use for the JSON output. Must conform to [JSON Schema](https://json-schema.org/).

prompt.description: The prompt to use for the JSON output

modes.description: The mode to use for change tracking. 'git-diff' provides a detailed diff, and 'json' compares extracted JSON data.

schema.description: Schema for JSON extraction when using 'json' mode. Defines the structure of data to extract and compare. Must conform to [JSON Schema](https://json-schema.org/).

prompt.description: Prompt to use for change tracking when using 'json' mode. If not provided, the default prompt will be used.

tag.description: Tag to use for change tracking. Tags can separate change tracking history into separate "branches", where change tracking with a specific tagwill only compare to scrapes made in the same tag. If not provided, the default tag (null) will be used.

oneOf.title: Audio
oneOf.description: Extract audio (MP3) from supported video URLs, e.g. YouTube. Returns a signed GCS URL.

oneOf.title: Video
oneOf.description: Extract best-quality video from supported video URLs, e.g. YouTube. Returns a signed GCS URL.

oneOf.title: Question
oneOf.description: Ask a natural-language question about the page. Returns the answer in the response `answer` field.

question.description: The question to answer about the page. Maximum 10,000 characters.

oneOf.title: Highlights
oneOf.description: Find relevant source text from the page. Returns the selected text in the response `highlights` field.

query.description: The text-selection query to run against the page. Maximum 10,000 characters.

Formats.description: Output formats to include in the response. You can specify one or more formats, either as strings (e.g., `'markdown'`) or as objects with additional options (e.g., `{ type: 'json', schema: {...} }`). Some formats require specific options to be set. Example: `['markdown', { type: 'json', schema: {...} }]`.

schema.description: The schema to use for the JSON output. Must conform to [JSON Schema](https://json-schema.org/).

prompt.description: The prompt to use for the JSON output

ParseFormats.description: Output formats supported for `/parse` uploads. Browser-rendering formats and change tracking are not supported.

ParseOptions.description: Optional parse options sent as JSON in the multipart `options` field.

onlyMainContent.description: Only return the main content of the page excluding headers, navs, footers, etc.

includeTags.description: Tags to include in the output.

excludeTags.description: Tags to exclude from the output.

headers.description: Headers to send when additional network requests are required.

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the request. Default is 30000 (30 seconds). Maximum is 300000 (300 seconds).

parsers.description: Controls file parser behavior when relevant (for example PDF parser mode).

mode.description: PDF parsing mode. "fast": text-only extraction. "auto": text-first with OCR fallback. "ocr": OCR on every page.

maxPages.description: Maximum number of pages to parse from the PDF.

skipTlsVerification.description: Skip TLS certificate verification when making requests.

removeBase64Images.description: Remove base64-encoded images from output and keep alt text placeholders.

blockAds.description: Enable ad and cookie popup blocking.

redactPII.description: Redact personally identifiable information from returned markdown. Pass `true` to use defaults, or an object to tune mode, entities, and replacement style.

proxy.description: Proxy mode for parse uploads. `/parse` supports only `basic` and `auto`.

origin.description: Origin identifier for analytics and logging.

zeroDataRetention.description: If true, this will enable zero data retention for this parse. To enable this feature, please contact help@firecrawl.dev

onlyMainContent.description: Only return the main content of the page excluding headers, navs, footers, etc. This is a deterministic HTML-level filter applied before markdown is generated; no LLM is involved.

onlyCleanContent.description: Beta. Run an additional LLM-based pass over the generated markdown to remove residual boilerplate that `onlyMainContent` can miss (cookie banners, ad blocks, social share widgets, breadcrumbs, newsletter signups, comment sections, related-article lists). Headings, lists, tables, code blocks, image references, and inline links are preserved. Can be combined with `onlyMainContent` (the most common setup) or used on its own. Skipped with a warning when the markdown exceeds the cleaning model's output token limit (the original markdown is preserved). Not supported on zero-data-retention requests.

includeTags.description: Tags to include in the output.

excludeTags.description: Tags to exclude from the output.

maxAge.description: Returns a cached version of the page if it is younger than this age in milliseconds. If a cached version of the page is older than this value, the page will be scraped. If you do not need extremely fresh data, enabling this can speed up your scrapes by 500%. Defaults to 2 days.

minAge.description: When set, the request only checks the cache and never triggers a fresh scrape. The value is in milliseconds and specifies the minimum age the cached data must be. If matching cached data exists, it is returned instantly. If no cached data is found, a 404 with error code SCRAPE_NO_CACHED_DATA is returned. Set to 1 to accept any cached data regardless of age.

headers.description: Headers to send with the request. Can be used to send cookies, user-agent, etc.

waitFor.description: Specify a delay in milliseconds before fetching the content, allowing the page sufficient time to load. This waiting time is in addition to Firecrawl's smart wait feature.

mobile.description: Set to true if you want to emulate scraping from a mobile device. Useful for testing responsive pages and taking mobile screenshots.

skipTlsVerification.description: Skip TLS certificate verification when making requests.

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the request. Minimum is 1000 (1 second). Default is 60000 (60 seconds). Maximum is 300000 (300 seconds).

parsers.description: Controls how files are processed during scraping. When "pdf" is included (default), the PDF content is extracted and converted to markdown format, with billing based on the number of pages (1 credit per page). When an empty array is passed, the PDF file is returned in base64 encoding with a flat rate of 1 credit for the entire PDF.

mode.description: PDF parsing mode. "fast": text-based extraction only (embedded text, fastest). "auto" (default): attempts fast extraction first, falls back to OCR if needed. "ocr": forces OCR parsing on every page.

maxPages.description: Maximum number of pages to parse from the PDF. Must be a positive integer up to 10000.

actions.description: Actions to perform on the page before grabbing the content

type.description: Wait for a specified amount of milliseconds
Wait for a specific element to appear
Take a screenshot. The links will be in the response's `actions.screenshots` array.

fullPage.description: Whether to capture a full-page screenshot (ignores viewport.height) or limit to the current viewport.

quality.description: The quality of the screenshot, from 1 to 100. 100 is the highest quality.

width.description: The width of the viewport in pixels

height.description: The height of the viewport in pixels

selector.description: Query selector to find the element by

all.description: Clicks all elements matched by the selector, not just the first one. Does not throw an error if no elements match the selector.

type.description: Write text into an input field, text area, or contenteditable element. Note: You must first focus the element using a 'click' action before writing. The text will be typed character by character to simulate keyboard input.

oneOf.title: Press a key
oneOf.description: Press a key on the page. See https://asawicki.info/nosense/doc/devices/keyboard/key_codes.html for key codes.

type.description: Press a key on the page
Scroll the page or a specific element

selector.description: Query selector for the element to scroll

type.description: Scrape the current page content, returns the url and the html.
Execute JavaScript code on the page
Generate a PDF of the current page. The PDF will be returned in the `actions.pdfs` array of the response.

format.description: The page size of the resulting PDF

landscape.description: Whether to generate the PDF in landscape orientation

scale.description: The scale multiplier of the resulting PDF

location.description: Location settings for the request. When specified, this will use an appropriate proxy if available and emulate the corresponding language and timezone settings. Defaults to 'US' if not specified.

languages.description: Preferred languages and locales for the request in order of priority. Defaults to the language of the specified location. See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Accept-Language

removeBase64Images.description: Removes all base 64 images from the markdown output, which may be overwhelmingly long. This does not affect html or rawHtml formats. The image's alt text remains in the output, but the URL is replaced with a placeholder.

blockAds.description: Enables ad-blocking and cookie popup blocking.

proxy.description:
Specifies the type of proxy to use.

 - **basic**: Proxies for scraping sites with none to basic anti-bot solutions. Fast and usually works.
 - **enhanced**: Enhanced proxies for scraping sites with advanced anti-bot solutions. Slower, but more reliable on certain sites. Costs up to 5 credits per request.
 - **auto**: Firecrawl will automatically retry scraping with enhanced proxies if the basic proxy fails. If the retry with enhanced is successful, 5 credits will be billed for the scrape. If the first attempt with basic is successful, only the regular cost will be billed.

storeInCache.description: If true, the page will be stored in the Firecrawl index and cache. Setting this to false is useful if your scraping activity may have data protection concerns. Using some parameters associated with sensitive scraping (e.g. actions, headers) will force this parameter to be false.

lockdown.description: If true, serves the request from Firecrawl's cache only and never makes an outbound request to the target URL. Designed for compliance-constrained or air-gapped environments where the scrape request itself could leak sensitive information. On cache miss, returns a 404 with error code SCRAPE_LOCKDOWN_CACHE_MISS (the URL is never logged on miss). Lockdown requests are treated as zero data retention. Default maxAge is extended to 2 years so existing cached pages remain eligible. Billed at 5 credits on hit, 1 credit on cache miss.

redactPII.description: Redact personally identifiable information from returned markdown. Pass `true` to use defaults, or an object to tune mode, entities, and replacement style.

profile.description: Enable persistent browser storage across scrape and interact sessions. Pass a profile when scraping to preserve cookies, localStorage, and session data. Sessions with the same profile name share browser state.

name.description: A name for the profile. Scrapes with the same name share browser state (cookies, localStorage, sessions).

saveChanges.description: When true, browser state is saved back to the profile when the interact session stops. Set to false to load existing data without writing. Only one saving session is allowed at a time.

summary.description: Summary of the page if `summary` is in `formats`

html.description: Cleaned HTML of the page if `html` is in `formats`. Removes `<script>`, `<style>`, `<noscript>`, `<meta>`, and `<head>` tags; converts relative URLs to absolute; resolves responsive image `srcset` to the largest version. Respects `onlyMainContent`, `includeTags`, and `excludeTags` filters.

rawHtml.description: The exact, unmodified HTML as received from the page if `rawHtml` is in `formats`. No cleaning or filtering is applied.

screenshot.description: Screenshot of the page if `screenshot` is in `formats`. Screenshots expire after 24 hours and can no longer be downloaded.

audio.description: Signed URL to the extracted MP3 audio file if `audio` is in `formats`. The signed URL expires after 1 hour.

video.description: Signed URL to the extracted video file if `video` is in `formats`. The signed URL expires after 1 hour.

answer.description: Natural-language answer to the question supplied via the `question` format. Only present if a `question` format object was included in `formats`.

highlights.description: Relevant source text selected by the `highlights` format. Only present if a `highlights` format object was included in `formats`.

links.description: List of links on the page if `links` is in `formats`

actions.description: Results of the actions specified in the `actions` parameter. Only present if the `actions` parameter was provided in the request

screenshots.description: Screenshot URLs, in the same order as the screenshot actions provided.

scrapes.description: Scrape contents, in the same order as the scrape actions provided.

javascriptReturns.description: JavaScript return values, in the same order as the executeJavascript actions provided.

pdfs.description: PDFs generated, in the same order as the pdf actions provided.

title.description: Title extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

description.description: Description extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

language.description: Language extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

sourceURL.description: The original URL that was requested. May differ from the page's final URL if redirects occurred.

url.description: The final URL of the page after all redirects have been followed.

keywords.description: Keywords extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

<any other metadata>.description: Other metadata extracted from HTML, can be a string or array of strings

statusCode.description: The status code of the page

contentType.description: The content type (MIME type) of the page, e.g. text/html, application/pdf

error.description: The error message of the page

concurrencyLimited.description: Whether this scrape was throttled due to team concurrency limits

concurrencyQueueDurationMs.description: Time in milliseconds the request waited in the concurrency queue. Only present when concurrencyLimited is true.

warning.description: Can be displayed when using LLM Extraction. Warning message will let you know any issues with the extraction.

changeTracking.description: Change tracking information if `changeTracking` is in `formats`. Only present when the `changeTracking` format is requested.

previousScrapeAt.description: The timestamp of the previous scrape that the current page is being compared against. Null if no previous scrape exists.

changeStatus.description: The result of the comparison between the two page versions. 'new' means this page did not exist before, 'same' means content has not changed, 'changed' means content has changed, 'removed' means the page was removed.

visibility.description: The visibility of the current page/URL. 'visible' means the URL was discovered through an organic route (links or sitemap), 'hidden' means the URL was discovered through memory from previous crawls.

diff.description: Git-style diff of changes when using 'git-diff' mode. Only present when the mode is set to 'git-diff'.

json.description: JSON comparison results when using 'json' mode. Only present when the mode is set to 'json'. This will emit a list of all the keys and their values from the `previous` and `current` scrapes based on the type defined in the `schema`. Example [here](/features/change-tracking)

branding.description: Branding information extracted from the page if `branding` is in `formats`. Includes colors, fonts, typography, spacing, components, and more.

colorScheme.description: The detected color scheme of the page.

colors.description: Brand colors extracted from the page.

fonts.description: Array of font families used on the page.

fontSizes.description: Font sizes for different text levels.

lineHeights.description: Line height values for different text types.

personality.description: Brand personality traits (tone, energy, target audience).

status.description: The current status of the crawl. Can be `scraping`, `completed`, or `failed`.

total.description: The total number of pages that were attempted to be crawled.

completed.description: The number of pages that have been successfully crawled.

creditsUsed.description: The number of credits used for the crawl.

expiresAt.description: The date and time when the crawl will expire.

next.description: The URL to retrieve the next 10MB of data. Returned if the crawl is not completed or if the response is larger than 10MB.

html.description: HTML version of the content on page if `includeHtml`  is true

rawHtml.description: Raw HTML content of the page if `includeRawHtml`  is true

links.description: List of links on the page if `includeLinks` is true

screenshot.description: Screenshot of the page if `includeScreenshot` is true

title.description: Title extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

description.description: Description extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

language.description: Language extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

sourceURL.description: The original URL that was requested. May differ from the page's final URL if redirects occurred.

url.description: The final URL of the page after all redirects have been followed.

keywords.description: Keywords extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

statusCode.description: The status code of the page

error.description: The error message of the page

concurrencyLimited.description: Whether this scrape was throttled due to team concurrency limits

concurrencyQueueDurationMs.description: Time in milliseconds the request waited in the concurrency queue. Only present when concurrencyLimited is true.

errors.description: Errored scrape jobs and error details

robotsBlocked.description: List of URLs that were attempted in scraping but were blocked by robots.txt

status.description: The current status of the batch scrape. Can be `scraping`, `completed`, or `failed`.

total.description: The total number of pages that were attempted to be scraped.

completed.description: The number of pages that have been successfully scraped.

creditsUsed.description: The number of credits used for the batch scrape.

expiresAt.description: The date and time when the batch scrape will expire.

next.description: The URL to retrieve the next 10MB of data. Returned if the batch scrape is not completed or if the response is larger than 10MB.

data.description: The data of the batch scrape.

html.description: HTML version of the content on page if `includeHtml`  is true

rawHtml.description: Raw HTML content of the page if `includeRawHtml`  is true

links.description: List of links on the page if `includeLinks` is true

screenshot.description: Screenshot of the page if `includeScreenshot` is true

title.description: Title extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

description.description: Description extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

language.description: Language extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

sourceURL.description: The original URL that was requested. May differ from the page's final URL if redirects occurred.

url.description: The final URL of the page after all redirects have been followed.

keywords.description: Keywords extracted from the page, can be a string or array of strings

statusCode.description: The status code of the page

error.description: The error message of the page

concurrencyLimited.description: Whether this scrape was throttled due to team concurrency limits

concurrencyQueueDurationMs.description: Time in milliseconds the request waited in the concurrency queue. Only present when concurrencyLimited is true.

invalidURLs.description: If ignoreInvalidURLs is true, this is an array containing the invalid URLs that were specified in the request. If there were no invalid URLs, this will be an empty array. If ignoreInvalidURLs is false, this field will be undefined.

title.description: The title of the page, if available.

description.description: A description of the page, if available.

invalidURLs.description: If ignoreInvalidURLs is true, this is an array containing the invalid URLs that were specified in the request. If there were no invalid URLs, this will be an empty array. If ignoreInvalidURLs is false, this field will be undefined.

status.description: The current status of the extract job

tokensUsed.description: The number of tokens used by the extract job. Only available if the job is completed.

SearchFeedbackRequest.description: Substantive feedback is required by rating: good requires valuableSources; partial requires valuableSources or missingContent; bad requires missingContent or querySuggestions.

question.description: Question or issue for the support agent to diagnose.

rationale.description: Optional context about what the end user is trying to accomplish.

fixParameters.description: Machine-readable API parameters that may fix the issue.

validation.description: Validation result when the support agent tested or attempted a fix.

feedback.description: Present when the support agent is blocked or needs more information.

durationMs.description: Total support-agent execution time in milliseconds.

answer.description: Concise answer grounded in Firecrawl documentation.

durationMs.description: Total docs-search execution time in milliseconds.

error.description: Support proxy or upstream error code.

RedactPIIEntity.description: Public PII entity buckets supported by Firecrawl redaction.

mode.description: Redaction strategy. `accurate` is model-only and optimized for precision, `aggressive` increases recall with additional heuristics, and `fast` uses heuristics without the model call.

entities.description: Restrict redaction to these entity buckets. If omitted, all supported entities are redacted.

replaceStyle.description: `tag` replaces spans with placeholders like `<EMAIL>`, `mask` replaces characters with `*`, and `remove` deletes the span text.
